title: Median of an even-length list
prompt: stats.py has a function median(xs) that returns the median of a list of numbers. It is wrong for even-length lists: it should return the average of the two middle values, but it returns only one of them. Fix median(xs) while keeping odd-length behavior correct.
prompt_vague: The median function has a bug. Fix it.
prompt_paraphrase: In stats.py, median(xs) is supposed to return the median of a list of numbers. For lists with an even number of elements it misbehaves: rather than averaging the two central values it returns just one of them. Please fix median(xs) without breaking the odd-length case.
